summary: Descriptive overview of votingrights in substantive law: fundamental right, restrictions, Shelby County impact.
key_points: fundamental right is a fundamental concept in constrights law.
Legal standards for votingrights are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine votingrights in litigation.
The doctrine of votingrights has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding votingrights requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
